records.m_pText: A Common Destination
They left…?$nLucky us, I suppose.
Are you alright?$nAre you hurt?
They were clearly after me.$nWhy did you not run?
Er, it didn’t cross my mind I guess…$nI was a little distracted.
But more importantly,$nare you hurt?
…I am fine.
You…you have my thanks.$nBut they will be back for me.$nI’m certain of it.
For your own sake, it is best$nour paths diverged here.
But…there’s only one road$nback to town.
Wha— Hey, wait!
The Miracle Man
Hey…
Hear me out. This isn’t the place$nto go charging on alone.
The crags are dark even by day.$nIt’s always been a nest for$nmonsters.
Airy, are you truly certain this$nboy is the miracle bringer?
Absolutely! Right time, right$nplace; he’s your miracle man.$nI’d bet my wings on it!
He saved your life, didn’t he?$nJust like my prediction said he$nwould!
I’d call that pretty darn$nmiraculous.
Yes, but…
Er…Mr. [PCM1], was it?
Just [PCM1] is fine.
[PCM1], then. Was it you$nthat my prayer reached?
I don’t know about “reached,”$nbut I heard you say it, anyway.
He’s the sole survivor of a$nvillage swallowed up whole.$nHow much more miracle$ndo you need?
That was no miracle…$nThere was nothing miraculous$nabout it…!
My family is gone. All my$nfriends. Everyone…$nThat was no miracle…
Rebuilding a Village
……$nI truly appreciate your$ndesire to help.
But…
Do you intend to just leave$nyour village like this?
No! Of course not.
I’ll rebuild it, someday.
I am wanted by the duchy.
Eternian assassins may lurk$nin any shadow, at any time.
What’s more, I must leave this$nplace at once to purify the$ncrystals.
If something were to befall you,$nwho would be left to put your$nvillage to rights again?
I’ll rebuild it… I will.
You don’t have to tell me$nI’m the only one who can.
Then surely it’s best you$nstayed and—
But Norende will never be$nwhole again until that$nchasm is gone.
And here I meet you, the one$nperson who knows how to make$nthat happen.
It seems the obvious thing to do$nis help you, keep you safe, and$nsolve this thing together.
Yes, but how do you propose—
I don’t know yet.
First, the king needs to know$nwhat’s happened.
[PCF1]’s Intentions
[PCF1]? What did you mean,$nyou’d “resolve the matter”?
Don’t tell me you’re thinking of$nstorming the Eternian camp at$nthe southwest lake alone…
Don’t be ridiculous.
I’m well aware that I am no match$nfor an airship.
Good. It sounds like they$nhave this city in their$ncrosshairs, besides.
As long as these people are$nheld hostage, I have no$nchoice.
They didn’t seem the types to$ntalk this out…
Wait… You wouldn’t. You can’t!
[PCM1] the Navigator
Your sense of direction is so$nbad it’s almost magical,$n[PCF1].
What? Surely it’s not so—
How many times did you$nget lost in that ravine?$nThere was only one path!
Then it took you two hours$nto find me at the top,$ndespite perfect visibility.
Er, the canyon path was…$nvery twisty. I’m certain it’s$na maze even to the locals…
Ha ha ha… Not really.
But at least this way I have an$nexcuse to tag along and…$nEr, never mind…
Stay close. The lake we make for$nis said to be unreachable by any$nbut the most knowledgeable.
R-right! Of course.
Mmm-hmm. I’ll bet.
So, um… What do you plan to do$nonce we get there, [PCF1]?
That is no concern of yours,$n[PCM1].
I’ve no choice…
Not as long as they have the$npeople of Caldisla in their$nsights.
It will be fine. Enemy or no,$nthey are men, not monsters.
Two Bumpkins
Hm? What’s wrong?$nYou seem distracted.
Hm? Oh, ah…
I’ve just never before walked$nthrough such a lively city$nas this…
Oh, really?
I don’t suppose it’s anything$nspecial for you…$nYou grew up here.
But to me, this is all—
Actually… Me, too.
I’m sorry?
I grew up in Norende. Norende$nhas…sheep. And grass.$nNothing like this…
It’s kind of overwhelming,$nto be honest.
So I think I can relate pretty$nwell, actually.
Oh… Oh, I see.$nWell, yes, then.
What parts jump out at you the$nmost? What’s the strangest?
Hmm, well… The number of$npeople, and buildings.
And the fact that they all seem$nso tranquil astounds me.
The seas are rotted, the duchy$nhas invaded, and still…
I suppose it’s a testament to$ntheir faith in the king’s rule.
A far cry from the kingdom I left…
Hm?
Ah! I’d nearly forgotten!
Owen informed me just before$nwe left.
Apparently the innkeep is$npreparing a special feast$nfor us this evening.
Best we did not keep him waiting.
Ooh, right!
A Man of Mysteries
[PCM1], might I…$nMight I have a word?
Sure. What’s on your mind?
That [PCM2] fellow.$nI’m just not…$nAre we truly certain…?
True, he doesn’t seem like the$nmost dependable guy.$nNice enough, though…
That isn’t my point!
He knew our names, [PCM1].$nBefore we’d ever spoken.
Right. He said they were in that$nbook of his or some such.
Our names alone is troubling$nenough…
But he knew I was the vestal$nas well!
What!?
Hmm, now that you mention it,$nhe knew we were out to seal$nthe chasm, too.
I’d noticed him mumbling$nstrange things in the streets$never since I arrived.
Not so strange as to give$nmilady offense, I hope?
Eep!
Er… Mr. [PCM2].
Please. No need for formality.
Would you tell us how$nyou knew [PCF1] was$nthe vestal of wind?
Hm? Oh, that.
All written in this journal,$nmy good man. Simple as that.
And would you mind if I had$na look at this journal?
By all means. Turn it over and$nshake it, if you like.$nSee what falls out.
And what is your tie to the girl$ntraveling with that pyromancer?
I’m to save her, apparently.
Don’t ask me how or why.$nI just know.
Save her?
You realize our errand at the$nCentro Ruins is to put an end to$nthe firestarter’s threat, yes?
Quite. And I understand the girl$nmay have something to say about$nit.
In any case, I’m confident I$nwon’t be a burden.
And I certainly have no intent$nof stopping you, if that’s$na concern.
Why don’t you hold on to the$njournal for now?
Investigate it to your$nsatisfaction.
And, once satisfied,$nI hope you’ll choose to$nplace your trust in me.
Well, I…I suppose we’ll see.
Talk of Titles
So, how should I address$neveryone from here on?
Going by the look of it,$nI’m the youngest one here…
Is it best if I stuck to sir$nand ma’am?
Please, just [PCM1].
…And [PCF1] for me,$nif you please.
Really? Even though you’re a$nvestal? Shouldn’t it be$nLady [PCF1] or something?
I would really rather you$ndidn’t…
Besides, it’s best we didn’t$ngo around shouting “vestal”$ntoo loudly.
Ooh, right. Almost forgot$nshe’s a wanted woman.
In that case, just [PCF1]$nis probably smartest.
Thank you.
Yes, that’s certainly wise.$nEspecially considering the$nvolume of our [PCF2]’s voice.
What’s wrong with my voice!
You said her voice was gentle$nas a spring breeze before,$n[PCM2].
Yes, well. Spring has a way of$ngiving over to summer.
And breezes build. Up close, it’s$nmore of a torna— Don’t think$nI’ll finish that thought.
Mrgrgrrr!
No, I think I’d like to hear this!$nSpeak up, [PCM2]!
Ah, I’ve remembered an errand.$nA very pressing, very distant$nerrand.
Hey! You get back here$nthis instant!
Ha ha ha…$nShe sure got on familiar terms$nwith [PCM2] pretty quick.
[PCF2]’s Misunderstanding
Owen…
……$nI can’t believe this!$nHow could Heinkel be so cruel?
What do we say to the innkeep?$nWhat can we say…?
Owen saved you after Norende$nfell. Took you in…
And since then, his father has$nshown you and me both a great$ndeal of kindness.
I’m no different. Owen trusted$nme, despite my circumstances.$nHelped me get by…
As for what to tell$nhis father…$nIt’s hard.
He deserves to know his son$ndied nobly. Fighting bravely.
I know that.$nI know…but what$nsmall comfort…
“Small comfort”…!?$nI think—
[PCF2], try to understand.$n[PCM1] and [PCM2] are$nonly thinking of the$ninnkeep’s feelings…
That’s exactly what I’m doing!
Is it not a comfort to know his$nson fell defending his king?
Enough. …Just forget it.
I’ll talk to him.
Let’s get moving.
[PCM1]…
A Father’s Care
It looked as if the innkeep gave$nyou something, [PCF2].
Mind if I ask what?
Hm? Oh, yes. I’m not sure.$nI’ll open it now.
Ooh, he packed us lunch!$nThe basket’s near bursting.
It looks amazing. And he made$nenough for all of us…
Mmm, looks like emperor trout$nsandwiches. Caldisla’s finest!$nWhat a treat!
He made one special for you,$n[PCF1].
“For the young lady who doesn’t$neat meat,” it says.
Grilled leaf buds and spring$nmushrooms…and apricot jelly$nfor dessert!
What did you get, [PCF2]?
Looks like I got what you did,$nplus the same dessert as [PCF1].$nAnd…
And a letter.
Yeah…
Hm? Let’s have a look.
“Your words were a great$ncomfort, my dear. Best of luck$nin battle, and best of health!”
…Alright. Let’s eat, then it’s off$nto save the king!
Right!
But You’re a Traitor
[PCF2], you said you’d get$nus VIP access, but are you$nreally sure?
Isn’t there a chance they’ll$nknow you’ve defected?
I don’t think there’s any$nworry of that yet.
If word had reached them, the$nroad to the villa would have$nbeen swarming with guards.
That’s how Heinkel operates.$nThorough and merciless.
I see. So, what of after we$nreach the villa, then?$nAny bright ideas?
If the man running the place$nis as careful as she says,$nwe can’t expect to—
Heh heh, I may not look it,$nbut I’m actually a fairly$nrespected figure.
I can talk our way past a couple$nof rank-and-file sentries no$nproblem.
A respected figure, eh?
I smell a story here.$nCare to elaborate?
Er, yes, well… You know.$nI just have a…a way about$nme, I suppose.
In that you’re a terror when$nyou’re mad, or that you rush in$nblind, or that you ignore$nauthority?
Excuse me! Isn’t that a$nlittle harsh, [PCM2]?$n…And all of the above.
A Hard Discovery
Er, [PCF1]?$nLook, I didn’t…
Try to understand, [PCF1].$n[PCF2] didn’t mean anything$nby it. She had to say that.
Right, exactly. It was just$na ploy to get us inside.$nAnd it worked, didn’t it?
But still. I didn’t have to$nbe so… I’m sorry!
No, it’s not that… I am not$nupset with you, [PCF2].
But the way they felt about the$nvestal…about the Orthodoxy.$nIt frightens me.
You spoke in horrific terms, and$nthey accepted it without$nquestion.
Which means that was in line$nwith their expectations of$nthe vestal…
Just what has happened in the$nduchy to cause this hatred?
Eternia was once the seat of$nthe Crystal Orthodoxy…
Can people’s hearts really$nchange so dramatically?
Still, I’m sorry.$nI didn’t have to say$nas much as I did.
You said enough, and I thank you$nfor it. Do not feel bad, please.
Alright. Time we all focused$non the task at hand!
Right!
[PCF2]’s Ire
Any guess where he’s headed,$n[PCF2]?
The villa rooftop.$nTo the airship berth!
Wasn’t this villa a retreat for$nthe Caldislan royal family?$nWhy would they have a berth?
I agree, it seems incongruous.
I use “berth” loosely. They’ve$ndriven an airship anchor into$nthe building’s roof.
Not much for finesse, are they?
We can discuss it later.$nNow, hurry! He’ll get away!
Don’t confuse haste for speed.$nWe need to be smart.
Right, I know…$nI know, but I just can’t$nforgive what Heinkel’s done.
He’s a knight who has ground$nchivalry into the dirt!
As I recall, knights are called$nto serve as the sword and$nshield of the weak.
Not to threaten the public, or$nburn down homes, or abduct$nroyalty…
I am in agreement with you,$n[PCF2]. This man is no knight.
Perhaps our views are more$nsimilar than I had dared believe.$nFor now, let us hurry.
To the roof, then!
About Ancheim
So, [PCF1]. Refresh my$nmemory—amnesia joke, by the$nway. Which way to Ancheim?
Er, yes, it’s…southeast…
So will this course serve, or…?
S-southeast…please.
Hm? My dear, I’m afraid I’ve no$nrecollection to go by. I need$nreliable directions, or—
(What should I do? The$ninnkeep said southeast$nand nothing more…)
(Ha ha, I think I see what$nhappened.)
[PCM2], she means it’s$nsoutheast of Caldisla,$nspecifically.
Aye aye!
Ooh, [PCF1]. Just what sort$nof place is Ancheim?
It’s…covered in a vast desert.$nAnd hot, as though it were$nalways midsummer.
Wow! That sounds like the$ncomplete opposite of where$nI was born.
Huh. I guess there’s all sorts$nof places out there.
This your first time leaving$nCaldisla, then, [PCM1]?
It is.$nWhat about you, [PCF2]?
Me? Er, I was a former Sky$nKnight! I’ve flown all$nover the world!
Where have you seen?
Oh, lots of places. Eternia,$nCaldisla…Caldisla.
Aw, you’re barely any better$nthan me.
The world is vast, my friends!$nThere are countries of$nperpetual summer,$nothers of winter…
Verdant lands rich in water,$nentire kingdoms built on$nvolcanoes, nations hidden$nin forests…
That sounds amazing!
…Or so I’d imagine.$nI’ve no memory, remember?
Whaaat? You’re just guessing?
Ah ha ha ha! The world is vast!$nKeep it that vague, and you’re$nsure to be right!
Ancheim’s Gourmand
Is anyone else hungry?$n[PCF1], what sort of food$nis Ancheim known for?
Hm? Oh. I’m afraid I so$nrarely left the temple…$nI couldn’t say.
And when I passed through on$nmy way to Caldisla, it was$nas a fugitive…
Well, just tell us about$nwhat you have eaten, then.
Hmmm… I seem to recall a great$ndeal of Ancheim’s cuisine being$nspicy.
One bite, and I felt as though I$nwere spewing flames. I broke$ninto a full-body sweat…
That sounds pretty intense.
Leave it to a desert people to$ndevelop a taste for searing$nheat.
I’m sure their women are no less$nfiery in—
Ooh, what about sweets!?$nAny cakes and things?
Ah! I’d nearly forgotten! I had$nthe most curious delicacy in a$nport on the inner sea.
Yes? Yes?
It…it was ice cream…$nth-that stretched!
Huh? Stretchy ice cream?$nThat sounds so weird!$nI can’t wait!
Get ready, weird stretchy ice$ncream—[PCF2]’s coming for$nyou! Full speed ahead!
Ha ha ha!
Temperament. Fiery in$ntemperament…
